serving.natural_language_portion: A typical restaurant serving of stir-fried prawn with Singapore chilli sauce is approximately 300 g, comprising 4–6 large prawns coated in a rich chilli-tomato-egg sauce, served with steamed white rice or mantou buns.

nutrient_source.title: Chilli Prawn (Singapore-style stir-fried prawn in chilli sauce) — nutrient profile estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for cooked shrimp (NDB 15152) and chilli-tomato sauce components, cross-referenced with HPB Singapore Foodo analogous seafood dish data
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / Health Promotion Board Singapore
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for 'Stir-Fried Prawn with Singapore Chilli Sauce' as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy cooked shrimp (NDB 15152: protein ~24 g, fat ~1.7 g per 100 g) with a chilli-tomato-egg sauce component (contributing carbohydrate, fat, sodium). HPB Singapore Foodo data for analogous chilli crab and seafood stir-fry dishes were used to calibrate sauce contribution and sodium levels. Final per-100g values represent the composite edible portion including sauce.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — seafood and mixed protein-dominant dishes
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Singapore chilli prawn stir-fry. GI estimated at 35 based on analogous high-protein, low-carbohydrate seafood dishes with chilli-tomato sauce from the University of Sydney GI Database. Protein-dominant dishes with moderate sauce carbohydrate typically fall in the GI 30–45 range. GL per serving calculated as (5.2 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 300 g serving / 100) × 35 / 100 = approximately 5.5.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~520 mg per 100 g; ~1560 mg per restaurant serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to half a restaurant serving and request reduced-sauce preparation, as a full serving provides approximately 1560 mg sodium — over 60% of the recommended daily limit of 2000 mg for hypertensive adults.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~35) and moderate carbohydrate content (~5.2 g per 100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: The low GI and high protein content of this dish make it a relatively favourable choice for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; however, the sauce contains added sugar and starch, so portion control and pairing with low-GI carbohydrate sides such as brown rice are recommended.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium and dietary cholesterol (~145 mg per 100 g) from prawns, partially offset by low saturated fat
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: While saturated fat is relatively low (~0.9 g per 100 g), the high sodium content and moderate dietary cholesterol from prawns warrant caution for individuals with cardiovascular disease; consuming a half-portion with steamed vegetables and requesting less sauce is advisable.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Stir-Fried Prawn with Singapore Chilli Sauce is a Singaporean Chinese seafood dish featuring large fresh prawns wok-fried at high heat and coated in a bold, semi-thick sauce made from fresh chillies, tomato paste, garlic, ginger, shallots, egg, soy sauce, and oyster sauce. The dish is a close relative of Singapore's iconic chilli crab, adapted to use whole prawns rather than crab. The prawns deliver a firm, succulent texture with a sweet oceanic flavour, while the sauce is simultaneously spicy, tangy, savoury, and slightly sweet. It is typically served with steamed white rice or deep-fried mantou buns to soak up the sauce. Nutritionally, the dish is high in lean protein (~14.5 g per 100 g), moderate in fat (~4.8 g per 100 g), and low in carbohydrate (~5.2 g per 100 g), giving it a low glycemic index of approximately 35. Sodium content is high (~520 mg per 100 g) due to the sauce, making it a concern for hypertensive individuals. The dish provides meaningful amounts of vitamin B12, selenium, phosphorus, and cholesterol from the prawns. It is halal-friendly and dairy-free but contains crustacean, egg, soy, and gluten allergens. Clinically, it is a reasonable protein source for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes due to its low GI, but sodium restriction is important for those with hypertension or cardiovascular disease.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for this exact composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy cooked shrimp data combined with HPB Singapore Foodo analogous chilli seafood dish data. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for protein-dominant seafood dishes. Confidence is medium rather than low because the estimation methodology is systematic and cross-referenced against two approved sources.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is -1 as it is not reliably reported for this composite dish in the source databases used; prawn contains trace vitamin D but the sauce contribution is negligible and no approved-source value is available for the composite. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy shrimp entry or HPB analogous dish data. Trans fat (trans_fat_g) is 0.0 as prawns and vegetable cooking oil used in stir-frying contain negligible industrially produced trans fats; confirmed negligible per USDA SR Legacy. GI estimated at 35 (low category, ≤55 threshold) based on analogous protein-dominant seafood dish from University of Sydney GI Database; no direct measurement available for this specific dish. Sodium is notably high due to oyster sauce, soy sauce, and chilli sauce components; restaurant preparation may vary. The cuisine_tags in the input JSON included 'Crab' and 'Lobster' which are not applicable to this prawn dish and have been disregarded. Price data from field survey data (SGD 30.80) is preserved as contextual metadata only and does not influence nutrient values.
